examples: Hath the rain a father? Or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
I will declare the decree: the LORD hath said unto me, Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee.
No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared him.
So, good luck, Cramer, with your crusade against executive profligacy begotten of fraud.
You could argue this is new language, begotten by the old but heard beyond the ancient barriers, and Thomas might agree language such as this might actually mean something.
